notes:
nVidia users cannot have mesa-vulkan* installed else the game will show the initial screen then load up a fullscreen, blacked out window; sometimes if you're lucky you'll crash at the menu. Black screen is from a hang that's occurring while the crash on the menu isn't recorded as an error of any sorts. On first ever launch with proton experimental the game actually loaded fully, it wasn't until after I started having these issues. 

My only lead right now after debugging Vulkan and adding a shared object to /usr/lib/ as my libGLX (Called by /usr/share/vulkan/ic.d/nvidia*) was located in /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/. After adding that, removing mesa, and checking vulkaninfo was I able to remove most of the errors and have Vulkan recognize my RTX 3060. There's still a single warning I'm debugging but I believe this issue is now a proton related one.
